title: AMA with @HyperliquidX - Decentralized Order Book built on Tendermint
summary_title: Order books on Tendermint: why Hyperliquid rebuilt DeFi from scratch
summary_description: Hyperliquid founder explains why they chose to build their own L1 instead of using existing infrastructure for decentralized perpetuals.

key_moments.label: Jeff introduces himself as Hyperliquid founder, background in market making
FTX implosion was the defining moment to build trustless platform
Why order books over AMMs and hybrid models like GMX
Key differentiator: entire order book runs on-chain, not centralized servers
Decision to build Hyperliquid chain on Tendermint after trading comp
Introducing vaults and pools for community-managed liquidity strategies
Mainnet timeline: testnet in January, closed alpha in February

pull_quote: The entire order book actually runs on chain. There's no one computer that if shut down, the whole thing stops.
